summary_details: The available data suggests the NM (Near Mint) market for this Pink Floyd — The Dark Side of the Moon pressing is moderately stable with a gradual upward trend over time. Most unsold listings cluster between roughly $170 and $195, while the single confirmed auction sale at $220.29 indicates that a motivated buyer may occasionally pay above the typical asking range. Current reasonable NM market value appears centered around the high-$170s to low-$200s depending on venue, urgency, and exact pressing desirability.
plan: Review all sales and unsold listings by condition and date, separate sold versus unsold behavior, identify clustering and trend direction, then estimate a reasonable current market range for each grade represented in the dataset.
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Identify which grades are present in the dataset; only NM appears.
Step 2: Separate unsold listings from completed auction/sold results because sold data carries more weight for realized market value.
Step 3: Observe chronological pricing trends from 2022 through 2026 to determine whether the market is rising, falling, or stable.
Step 4: Compare platform behavior across Amazon and Discogs to identify common listing ranges.
Step 5: Use clustering of repeated unsold listings around $170–$195 and the lone successful auction at $220.29 to estimate a balanced current NM range.
Step 6: Widen the range slightly because confirmed completed sales data is sparse.
evidence: Most NM unsold listings from 2023–2026 cluster between approximately $174 and $195 across Amazon and Discogs.
The only confirmed completed auction sale reached $220.29 in January 2026, establishing an upper-end realized benchmark.
Pricing generally trends upward from 2022 lows near $140–$155 toward later listings consistently near or above $175.
assumptions: All entries represent the exact same pressing and comparable completeness/packaging condition.
Unsold listing prices still reflect seller expectations and approximate market sentiment.
The January 2026 auction was a legitimate arms-length transaction without unusual bidding behavior.
limitations: The dataset contains only one confirmed sold/auction result, limiting confidence in realized value estimates.
No lower grades such as VG or VG+ are present, so no cross-grade relationship can be inferred.
Pressing-specific details, regional variants, inserts, and matrix numbers are not provided and may materially affect value.
alternative_interpretations: The repeated unsold listings near $180–$195 may indicate seller overpricing rather than true market value.
The $220.29 auction result may represent an outlier caused by scarcity, bidding competition, or exceptional presentation.
The apparent upward trend could partly reflect broader inflation and collectible market expansion rather than increased rarity.
